description: This figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns of as-cast Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-xCr low-density steel strips, with x values of 0, 3, 6, 9 wt.% corresponding to samples labeled 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr, 9Cr respectively. The x-axis represents 2θ ranging from 30° to 100°, while the y-axis denotes diffraction intensity in arbitrary units (a.u.). All observed peaks are indexed to two phases: face-centered cubic austenite (γ) with corresponding crystallographic planes (111), (200), (220), (311), (222), and body-centered cubic δ-ferrite with planes (110), (200), (211). As Cr content increases, the intensity of the δ(110) peak at 2θ = 44.1° gradually rises, proving that Cr addition promotes δ-ferrite formation. Calculated austenite lattice constants decrease from 3.70 Å for 0Cr to 3.55 Å for 9Cr. This figure confirms that austenite is the major matrix phase for all as-cast samples, and Cr addition effectively increases ferrite fraction in the alloy.
